explicit_text: I was born in Colorado Springs, Colorado, but my mother died shortly after my birth. I lived there with my father and siblings for less than three years before being sent away. It was the starting point of my early life before I moved on to other places for education and work.

explicit_text: In 1899, the De La Vergne family sent me to the Hampton Institute in Virginia, where I graduated in 1904. Despite the challenges, I completed my education there.

explicit_text: After graduating, I moved with my father to Oklahoma, then Indian Territory, and settled in Tulsa. explicit_text: The Lincoln Motion Picture Company was organized in Los Angeles in 1915 by my brother, Noble Johnson. I joined in 1916 as general booking and publicity manager, working from Omaha to expand the company's reach nationally. explicit_text: After the Lincoln Motion Picture Company disbanded in 1923, I established the Pacific Coast News Bureau, which operated until 1927. It provided Negro news of national importance at lower rates than other press associations. Many leading Negro journals subscribed to the service, and I often wrote under the name George Perry.

entity: George P. Johnson Negro Film Collection
explicit_text: I began collecting materials on Negro contributions to films around the time I joined the Lincoln Motion Picture Company. The George P. Johnson Negro Film Collection includes correspondence, clippings, photographs, and documents that preserve the history of Negro actors and film companies. It is a unique archive that identifies many uncredited Negro actors and provides valuable research material.
